headlineSummary: Anthropic and OpenAI drew negative Hacker News sentiment in a close tie, with Anthropic criticized more often across quality and pricing issues [E1][E2][E3][E5] and OpenAI hit by a severe privacy complaint with limited evidence [E6].

ranking.summary: OpenAI was negative on limited evidence because one comment alleged Azure OpenAI leaked another customer’s prompt responses under load [E6].
Anthropic skewed negative on repeated complaints about Claude-related quality, accuracy, and pricing, despite one positive note about Sonnet 4.5’s capabilities [E1][E2][E3][E4][E5].

evidence.excerpt: I have it so every repo has its own long-lived agent. They do get excited and start changing other repos, which messes up memory. I didn't want to create a system user per repo because that's obnoxious, so I created a single claude system user, and I am using the virtual file system to manage permissions.

annotations.rationale: The comment describes the agent behavior as problematic and explicitly ties the setup to a "claude system user," indicating criticism of Claude-based agent behavior.

evidence.excerpt: With the Claude bug, or so it is known, burning through tokens at record speed, I gave alternative models a try and they're mostly ... interchangeable.

annotations.rationale: The commenter criticizes Claude for rapidly consuming tokens, a negative statement about cost efficiency.

evidence.excerpt: If I ran this on Sonnet, we’re talking thousands per month. With DeepSeek, it’s more like hundreds.

annotations.rationale: The commenter says Sonnet would cost "thousands per month," which is a clear negative on price.

evidence.excerpt: I know that no model right now matches the full performance and capabilities of Claude Sonnet 4.5

annotations.rationale: The commenter states that no current model matches Claude Sonnet 4.5's performance and capabilities, which is explicit praise.

evidence.excerpt: Seen two others so far and they've all had major inaccuracies either regarding trajectory, current distance, or current mission state. One even said the remaining mission time was over 400 days. They all obviously used Claude Code.

annotations.rationale: The commenter blames "Claude Code" for trackers with "major inaccuracies," clearly criticizing output quality.

evidence.excerpt: I've seen Azure OpenAI leak other customer's prompt responses to us under heavy load. https://x.com/DaveManouchehri/status/2037001748489949388 Nobody seems to care.

annotations.rationale: The commenter alleges that the named Azure OpenAI service leaked another customer's prompt responses, which is severe criticism on privacy/security grounds.
